questions.stem: The block diagram of a system is shown below (multi-loop system with blocks G1, G2, G3, G4, G5, G6, feedback paths H1, H2, and external input X(s)). Using block diagram reduction techniques, find all the possible transfer functions.
common_errors: Missing one of the multiple outputs or ignoring the X(s) disturbance input
Incorrect sign at summing junctions after moving blocks past them
Not identifying all independent transfer functions (C/R, C/X)
questions.solution_method: Systematically eliminate inner loops first (G5 inner loop), then reduce the H1 and H2 feedback loops step by step using standard BDR rules. Identify all input-output pairs (R→C and X→C) and apply Mason-equivalent or algebraic reduction for each.
key_formulas: T = G/(1 + GH) for single feedback loop

questions.stem: A temperature control industrial heating system is described with: steam flow rate Q m³/s, heat produced proportional to Q (Kh=1), feed temperature Ti, product temperature T, thermal capacitance C=5 J/°C, thermal resistance Rt=2 °C/W, first-order sensor TF Kt/(τt·s+1) with Kt=5mA/°C and τt=2s, I-to-P converter (0-16mA to 0-12psi), linear control valve Kv=1 m³s⁻¹psi⁻¹. Draw the block diagram of the entire system showing TF of each block.
common_errors: Incorrect thermal plant TF — forgetting Rt in the denominator (plant TF is Rt/(RtCs+1))
Wrong I-to-P gain calculation (12psi/16mA = 0.75 psi/mA)
Missing disturbance path from Ti
questions.solution_method: Model thermal tank as first-order: ΔT(s)/ΔQ(s) = Kh·Rt/(RtCs+1) = 2/(10s+1). I-to-P gain = 12/16 = 0.75 psi/mA. Chain blocks: Ir → I-to-P (0.75) → valve (Kv=1) → plant (2/(10s+1)) → sensor (5/(2s+1)) → back to summing junction.

questions.stem: For the temperature control system in Q2, derive the overall transfer function ΔT(s)/ΔIr(s).
common_errors: Arithmetic error multiplying gains through the forward path
Forgetting to include Kv in forward path

questions.stem: For the temperature control system in Q2, determine the sensitivity of the closed loop transfer function with respect to Kt for ω=10 rad/s.
common_errors: Confusing sensitivity w.r.t. plant vs. sensor parameter
Not evaluating numerically at ω=10 rad/s
Sign error in sensitivity formula
questions.solution_method: Use S = (∂T/∂Kt)·(Kt/T). Derive CLTF as function of Kt, differentiate, substitute jω=j10. Alternatively use S_H = -GH/(1+GH) evaluated at s=j10.
key_formulas: S = -GH/(1+GH) for sensitivity to feedback element

questions.stem: For the temperature control system in Q2, determine the steady-state error for unit step input.
common_errors: Using ess = 1/(1+Kp) without checking units/gain chain
Forgetting sensor gain Kt in the DC loop gain
questions.solution_method: Find DC loop gain L(0) = G(0)·H(0) = 1.5 × 5 = 7.5. Type-0 system so Kp=7.5. For unit step: ess = 1/(1+Kp) = 1/8.5.
key_formulas: ess = 1/(1 + Kp) for Type-0, step input

questions.stem: The open loop transfer function of a negative feedback system is G(s)H(s) = K(s+1)/[s(s-1)(s+10)]. Draw the Nyquist plot and find the range or value of K for closed loop system stability.
common_errors: Incorrect counting of open-loop RHP poles (P=1 for pole at s=+1)
Missing indentation around origin pole
Wrong encirclement count direction leading to incorrect stability condition
questions.solution_method: Note OLTF has one open-loop RHP pole (s=+1), so P=1 and need N=-1 for stability (Z=N+P=0 requires N=-1, i.e., one CCW encirclement of -1/K). Sketch Nyquist contour: find real-axis crossings of GH(jω), determine the value of ω where Im[GH]=0, compute Re[GH] there. Apply Nyquist criterion to find range of K for N=-1.
key_formulas: Z = N + P, need Z=0 for stability
Set Im[GH(jω)]=0 to find phase crossover

questions.stem: Plant TF = 4/(2s+1). A proportional (gain K) and integral (gain Ki) controller is added so that damping ratio ζ=0.5 and settling time (2% band) < 0.1 s. Find the range of K and Ki.
common_errors: Using ts = 4/(ζωn) without checking which formula applies (2% vs 5% band)
Not treating K and Ki as two unknowns and solving the system of equations from ζ and ts constraints
Ignoring the additional pole at origin introduced by integrator
questions.solution_method: OLTF with PI = K(1 + Ki/s)·4/(2s+1). CE becomes 3rd order. For dominant 2nd-order approximation: ζ=0.5 and ts=4/(ζωn)<0.1 gives ωn>80 rad/s. Match CE coefficients to desired pole locations to find K and Ki ranges.

questions.stem: With values of K and Ki obtained in Q4(a), find the range of steady-state error for unit ramp input.
common_errors: Forgetting that PI controller makes system Type-1 (zero sse for ramp)
Computing Kv incorrectly when K range is a set of values
questions.solution_method: PI controller introduces an integrator, making the forward path Type-1. For ramp input: ess = 1/Kv where Kv = lim_{s→0} s·G_OL(s). Compute Kv = 4KKi/1 and substitute range of K, Ki from part (a).

questions.stem: The characteristic equation of a negative feedback system is (s²+2s)(s²+2s+5) + Ks + K = 0, 0<K<∞. Sketch the root locus clearly showing all steps involved.
common_errors: Incorrect factoring of the K-independent part to identify open-loop poles/zeros
Missing a zero at s=0 or misidentifying the zero
Asymptote angle calculation error for 4 poles and 1 zero (3 asymptotes)
questions.solution_method: Rewrite CE as G(s)H(s) = K(s+1) / [s(s+2)(s²+2s+5)] = -1. Identify OL poles: s=0, s=-2, s=-1±j2; OL zero: s=-1. Number of asymptotes = 4-1=3, angles = 60°,180°,300°. Find centroid = (0-2-1-1+1-(-1))/3. Check breakaway points, jω-axis crossing via RH on CE.

questions.stem: The open loop transfer function of a negative feedback system is G(s)H(s) = 100K(s+5)(s+40)/[s³(s+100)(s+200)]. Sketch the Bode plots and determine the range of K for closed loop system to be stable. Start plots at K=1 and ω=1 rad/s.
common_errors: Incorrect initial slope — missing that s³ gives −60 dB/decade start
Wrong DC gain computation for magnitude plot starting point at ω=1
Forgetting to check both GM and PM for stability condition
questions.solution_method: At K=1: DC gain factor = 100×5×40/(100×200)=1, so 0 dB at ω=1 before slope. Start at −60dB/decade (triple integrator). Add slope breaks at ω=5(+20),40(+20),100(−20),200(−20). Find phase crossover frequency ωpc where ∠GH=−180°, compute |GH(jωpc)| to get GM. Stability requires K < GM_critical.

notes: 2-hour closed-book exam, MM=90. Q2 is a 4-part question (2a–2d) totalling 15 marks; sub-mark breakdown not printed. Q4 is 2-part totalling 15 marks. Q3=18, Q5=15, Q6=17, Q1=10. Total matches 90. No state-space or discrete-time questions.
